title: DORA Exit Strategy — Alternative Provider Shortlist Currency and RFP Readiness

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.5 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.5 (ICT risk-management governance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.35: DORA Exit Strategy — Alternative Provider Shortlist Currency and RFP Readiness.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.35' running on dora_exit_shortlist.csv (critical_service, alt_provider_id, last_rfp_contact, nda_expiry, market_scan_date), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
| inputlookup dora_exit_shortlist.csv
| eval nda_exp=strptime(nda_expiry,"%Y-%m-%d"), scan=strptime(market_scan_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where nda_exp<relative_time(now(),"+90d@d") OR scan<relative_time(now(),"-365d@d")
| stats values(alt_provider_id) as providers by critical_service
| sort critical_service
description: Exit strategies require credible alternatives. Tracking age of competitive shortlists, NDA status, and last market scan shows the entity refreshes exit options rather than relying on a stale single name in a slide deck.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: KV Store / CSV from procurement.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `dora_exit_shortlist.csv` (critical_service, alt_provider_id, last_rfp_contact, nda_expiry, market_scan_date).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Link `critical_service` to BIA; (2) procurement owns updates; (3) integrate CLM for NDA dates; (4) exclude services with regulated monopoly constraints via notes field; (5) board risk committee review annually.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup dora_exit_shortlist.csv
| eval nda_exp=strptime(nda_expiry,"%Y-%m-%d"), scan=strptime(market_scan_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where nda_exp<relative_time(now(),"+90d@d") OR scan<relative_time(now(),"-365d@d")
| stats values(alt_provider_id) as providers by critical_service
| sort critical_service
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA Exit Strategy — Alternative Provider Shortlist Currency and RFP Readiness** — Exit strategies require credible alternatives. Tracking age of competitive shortlists, NDA status, and last market scan shows the entity refreshes exit options rather than relying on a stale single name in a slide deck.

Documented **Data sources**: `dora_exit_shortlist.csv` (critical_service, alt_provider_id, last_rfp_contact, nda_expiry, market_scan_date). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): KV Store / CSV from procurement. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **nda_exp** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where nda_exp<relative_time(now(),"+90d@d") OR scan<relative_time(now(),"-365d@d")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by critical_service** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (stale shortlists), Timeline (market_scan_date), Single value (NDAs expiring).
